fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Massachusetts Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Massachusetts - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity massachusetts, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to massachusetts, pharmacy technician compact massachusetts, massachusetts pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Massachusetts Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address:
Board of Registration in Pharmacy
250 Washington Street
Boston, MA 02108

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Massachusetts.

steps.title: 1. Review Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you meet the minimum age of 16 years and satisfy the education requirements: either hold a high school diploma or its equivalent, be currently enrolled in a program leading to such, or be certified by the Pharmacy Technician Certification Board (PTCB) or the National Healthcareer Association (NHA).

steps.title: 2. Register for a Gateway Account
steps.description: Visit the Professional Licensure Gateway at https://mass.gov/gateway and register for a new account. You will need to create a username and password.

steps.title: 3. Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log into your Professional Licensure Gateway account. Select the 'Board of Registration in Pharmacy' and then initiate the 'Pharmacy Technician Application'. Fill out all required sections completely, including attesting to any criminal history.

steps.title: 4. Upload Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Electronically upload all necessary supporting documents through the Gateway. This typically includes proof of age (e.g., driver's license, birth certificate) and proof of education or certification (e.g., high school diploma, GED certificate, or PTCB/NHA certification).

steps.title: 5. Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $100.00 through the Professional Licensure Gateway using an accepted payment method.

steps.title: 6. Await Board Review and Approval
steps.description: After submitting your complete application and fee, the Board of Registration in Pharmacy will review your submission. You will be notified of your application status or any further requirements through the Gateway or via mail.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Massachusetts pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Massachusetts have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Massachusetts. To work in Massachusetts, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Massachusetts?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Massachusetts while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Massachusetts
hero.dek: Apply online through the Massachusetts Professional Licensure Gateway (mass.gov/gateway). Create an account, locate the Pharmacy Technician application under the Board of Registration in Pharmacy, and submit all required documentation.

variants.label: New Applicant

variants.label: Reciprocity

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: First-time applicants must register for a new account on the Gateway. This involves creating a username and password, then verifying your account before you can log in and start an application.
